sections.paragraph: an unused root, prob. i.q. Arab. سَنَّ (kindred to the Hebrew שָׁנַן), to form, to put into shape; whence سُنَّةُ form, appearance, سَنَنُ rule, mode. Hence Heb. זַן kind, species (the origin of which has hitherto been unknown to etymologists); although this word afterwards, its origin being neglected, was inflected according to the analogy of verbs לה֞.
